entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: This course will provide the ½ credit health requirement for those states that require it or elective credit for any student (two terms of lessons). Approached within a Charlotte Mason framework of focusing on the whole personhood of the student, this course will explore nutrition, mental health, and physical well-being.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: May be suitable for Grade 9 students

rows.label: Health Elective (Beta)
rows.books: A Simply Healthy Life||Body into Balance: An Herbal Guide to Holistic Self-Care||The Joy of Movement||Eat Well and Feel Great

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Health Elective (Beta)
☐ At the beginning of the year, students will need to set up a health notebook of some sort where they will record their personal takeaways after each reading session. A wellness journal is highly recommended but not required to help track each student’s health journey. An optional link to purchase is included in the Quick Links.

☐ As you move through the book Body into Balance, make note of any of the recipes that Maria shares that you would like to attempt in Term 3. Keep a running list and order the supplies you’ll need by the end of Term 2. Recommended herb suppliers include Mountain Rose Herbs and Starwest Botanicals.

entries.title: Term Prep & Teacher Tips
entries.content:
Health Elective (Beta)
Term 2: Look through the Quick Link options or choose your own plan to reach your Intentional Fitness Goal by the end of the term.

books.title: Body into Balance: An Herbal Guide to Holistic Self-Care

blocks.text: The lesson plans will guide you through what to read or watch over the terms. Take a moment before reading/watching to remember where you left off last time.
The lesson plans will give you specific instructions or tell you to choose a way to narrate that helps you interact with the ideas.
The lesson plans will guide you with questions or instructions to share your ideas or what you are reading with others.

These lessons are written directly to students, but to assist them throughout the year, please read through this section and the lesson plans so you are familiar with the instructions and flow of the lessons. Accountability components are occasionally added to facilitate teacher/student discussion and growing independence. 

It is also valuable for those in a classroom setting to have a mix of lessons that students complete themselves silently and others read aloud and narrate together. These lessons lend themselves well to a larger discussion time at the end of the lesson where students can share their ideas.

lessons.title: 45m Health Elective (Beta) - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Video links

➜ INTRO
Throughout this course, you'll explore a wide range of ideas about health, from traditional medicine to herbal remedies and holistic care. You’ll hear a variety of ideas, think critically about them, and decide what makes sense based on evidence and experience. The goal isn’t to all think the same way, but to engage thoughtfully, ask good questions, and learn how to make wise, informed choices.

The video in these first two lessons challenges traditional medicine as the only route to health. While Alveary does not advocate the specific stances taken or the books the speakers wrote, it is an interesting opening to discuss the role of cell health, diet, lifestyle, metabolic differences, inflammation, etc., as factors in our long-term health. 

During the first lesson each week, after watching the videos or reading the articles assigned, write a narration and include your takeaways. (How will you apply what you learned to your health journey?)

➜ VIEW
∞ Video Link: What is Health? (Stop at 2:00.)

➜ LISTEN & NARRATE
Listen to the first twenty minutes, record your takeaways, and discuss the following questions: 
• How does this conversation differ from other thoughts you've heard on this subject? 
• What parts do you agree or disagree with? 
• What is the balance between the roles of traditional medicine and holistic medicine?
∞ Video Link: Dr. Josh Axe: Is the Healthcare System Designed to Keep You Sick? (51:56)

links.text: Dr. Josh Axe: Is the Healthcare System Designed to Keep You Sick?

lessons.title: 45m Health Elective (Beta) - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: A Simply Healthy Life

➜ INTRO
During the second lesson each week, after reading the assignment for A Simply Healthy Life, write a narration and include your takeaways.  (How will you apply what you learned to your health journey?) Each chapter presents challenges to help you on your health journey. You won't be able to do one each week, but I highly encourage you to choose one to complete and journal your results at some point in the term.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.1 "Changing Your Life for Good"

• Notice the quotes at the beginning of each chapter…do any resonate with you? Consider adding them to your commonplace book.
• Do you have a goal you're working towards? If you do, complete the High-Yield Habit Exercise (write this in your health notebook). If you don't have a big goal you’re working towards right now, choose 1 of the Habit Challenges to complete. Keep track of your habit over the next term with a habit tracker that you can put into your health journal (this can be as simple or as complicated as you want … just search “habit trackers” to see some examples…there are even free apps to track your habits!).

lessons.title: 45m Health Elective (Beta) - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Body Into Balance

➜ INTRO        
The third lesson each week will include additional reflection questions to help you make connections between the material you read/watch and your health journey. We will also use the third lesson each week to cover the book Body Into Balance, where you’ll be introduced to herbal medicine. Looking for additional herbal resource books? Consider Rosemary Gladstar’s Medicinal Herbs: A Beginner’s Guide, or Rosalee de la Foret’s Alchemy of Herbs. Looking for a historically scientific peek into American herbal medicine? Consider American Eden: David Hosack, Botany, and Medicine in the Garden of the Early Republic by Victoria Johnson.

➜ NOTEBOOK
What’s your definition of health? What is one bad habit you’d like to break? Is there a good habit you could replace the bad habit with that would fulfill the same reward or desire?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Introduction

• Add the 4 Safety Rules for Using Herbs to your health notebook.

lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Body Into Balance

➜ NOTEBOOK
How does food impact our mental health?

➜ ACTIVITY
Take inventory of your personal care products. Which ones might you choose to investigate or gradually swap out? Come up with a plan!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.31
As we now get into specific herbs, you may either choose to read about all of the herbs presented, or you may choose one herb to read more about. The assignments all assume that you’re going to choose one to learn more about, but don’t limit yourself! You also will not be assigned the reading on Protocol Points, but if you see something that applies to your health journey, feel free to follow that detour!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Choose one nutritive herb (p.33-41) to read about and record what you learned in your health notebook. (You can even treat this assignment as a nature study reflection…draw the plant, label it, and record what it can be used for.)

lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Joy of Movement

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.109-123 "At the 2017" - "to the past."

• “When you let yourself be moved by music, you lay down tracks in your nervous system, pathways for joy to traverse when you hear that song again. I know that when I choose to dance today, I am building muscle memories of joy, giving my future self more songs to be moved by” (p.122). Pick a song you like and MOVE to it. Create neural pathways and allow the music to move your body and express joy.

p.179-195 "Shawn Bearden, then" - "this! Keep striving."

• The word "athlete" comes from a Greek word for “I struggle, I suffer." How do you consider that when faced with a task, like exercise, that requires endurance? Is it possible for joy and suffering to coexist?

lessons.title: 45m Health Elective (Beta) - Lesson 75
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Body Into Balance

➜ NOTEBOOK
Reflecting on the idea that you require more nutrients in your teenage years than at any other time in your life, and considering what you read about eating a rainbow, what are some of the foods that you currently eat that you can swap out to fulfill your need for more nutrients?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Everyone read p.233-234 (stop at the bottom of the first column).  Gentlemen, go on to read p.234-240.  Ladies, skip ahead to read p.242-249 and then applicable parts of p.249-257 (you don’t need to read all of this…find the parts that apply to your cycle).

➜ NOTE
Be sure to make notes of your takeaways…maybe you don’t have any of these issues yet, but make a neural pathway now by narrating so that if you ever experience any of these issues, you’ll remember that you learned about herbs that might help.

lessons.title: 45m Health Elective (Beta) - Lesson 78
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: Body Into Balance

➜ NOTEBOOK
This week, you read about two of the most important concepts in nutrition: the importance of healthy fats in the diet and balancing your blood sugar. These are two keys for the brain, mental health, moods, and wellbeing. It’s so easy to swap out sugar and refined white carbs with healthier options. Read back over the suggestions in the book…what changes can you make to your diet to move towards a healthier nutritional journey?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.259, 261-262

• Choose an herb that fights free radicals (p.263-265) to read about and record what you learned in your health notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Chapter 17 is about herbs with children and animals. If you have younger siblings or pets, this chapter will have relevant information. It’s all fascinating, so even if you don’t have younger siblings or pets, maybe one day you’ll need this information! Everyone should read p.277-278, and then you can skim over the remaining pages and read what interests you.

Follow Maria’s instructions on p.302-303, or watch these videos to see how to make infusions and decoctions.

lessons.body:
Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Health Elective (Beta)

terms.content:
· What are the ways sleep affects our health?

· How does the reconstruction of the teenage brain affect teen behavior?

· Explain the connection between the gut and mental health.

· Tell what you know about how to cultivate a simply healthy life.

· Choose a special topic we covered this term: time spent in nature, drugs and alcohol, or stress, and tell how that topic relates to health.

· Describe what tools and takeaways you can use to apply to your own health journey.

· Tell how exercise can help us find happiness, hope, courage, and connection.

· Tell how you can manage your diet to optimally support your mood, growth, and the changes you’re experiencing as a teen. What foods should you include? What foods and drinks should you exclude?

· Describe the gut-brain axis and tell how you can support mental health by supporting your microbiome. What kinds of foods support a healthy microbiome?
